message:
feat(core): add quantile latency budgets, adaptive transport policy, and mission schema

Three major extensions to the core pipeline modeling and planning systems:

latency_model.rs (+705 lines):
- QuantileBudgetMs: typed quantile latency budgets (p50/p95/p99/p999) with
  validation enforcing non-negative, finite, monotonic constraints
- Budget arithmetic: element-wise sum, max, scalar multiply, and
  worst-case composition for pipeline stage aggregation
- BudgetContract and BudgetContractError: declarative SLO contracts that
  can be validated against observed latency distributions
- Per-stage and per-pipeline budget decomposition with headroom tracking

latency_stages.rs (+332 lines):
- Adaptive transport policy (C4) with three modes: Local (zero-copy/memcpy),
  Compressed (zstd/lz4 framing), and Bypass (small/already-compact data)
- TransportCostModel: parameterized cost model for mode selection based on
  compression CPU cost, decompression cost, network cost, and expected ratios
- Deterministic mode selection given the same cost model inputs
- TransportPolicyEngine with per-stage override support and metrics tracking

plan.rs (+819 lines):
- Mission Schema Pack (MISSION_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1): stable identifiers for
  missions, candidate actions, and assignments using content-addressable hashes
- MissionNouns: structured mission decomposition with typed phases and gates
- Ownership contracts: agent assignment tracking with handoff semantics,
  capability requirements, and deadline enforcement
- Integration with existing OnFailure and plan execution infrastructure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
